title: Skill World Model / Agentic Manipulation API
description: Keep in Survey: the Notion Skill World Model and Agentic API ideas are still open, but need a precise boundary between skill memory, interaction dynamics, and tool/API orchestration.
summary: Survey-stage migration of the Notion Skill World Model, Agentic 4 General Manipulation, and interaction-centric dynamics notes: define whether a reusable manipulation skill memory should store demos, object/contact transitions, tactile/force evidence, API calls, or all of them, and choose the first benchmark before it becomes engineering.

details.text: Core Notion idea: Skill World Model should model reusable manipulation skills as state/action/contact transitions, not only generated video. The live research question is what the minimal skill primitive is: object-centric transition, contact field, reward/failure label, or callable API/tool.

Agentic API pipeline / Retrieval-Augmented Manipulation: use retrieval over prior demos, object affordances, and learned action snippets as an API-like layer for general manipulation. This belongs in Survey until the retrieval key, memory schema, and fallback policy are explicit.

Interaction-centric task-library notes split the signal into affordance before interaction, object trajectory after interaction, and force/contact as the bridge. That gives this card a concrete data schema direction and connects it to VTLA rather than creating another abstract world-model topic.

Random Thought entries folded here: tactile/force dynamic model, human-robot perception alignment, point-cloud fast rollout, spilling-water quantitative tasks, RobotCooking as a later stress test, and skill libraries that can be reused across robots and simulators.

Relevant tactile/video foundations include ViTacFormer for future contact prediction, ViTaSCOPE for object-centric contact fields, and Universal Visuo-Tactile Video Understanding for video-level tactile semantics. These solve representation pieces but not the dashboard-level skill memory/API boundary.

risks_decisions: Do not promote this into Research until it chooses one first skill primitive and one benchmark family; otherwise it duplicates UMI, VTLA, and Self-Improving Agents.
A useful skill memory must be executable or evaluable. A list of paper links or natural-language skill names is not enough.
The Agentic API angle should specify what is retrieved, what is generated, what is executed, and what happens when the skill is missing or low confidence.
Next executable analysis: write a 1-page spec comparing three memory schemas: demo retrieval, object/contact transition graph, and callable skill API with verifier outputs.
Survey disposition 2026-05-31: keep, because no current dashboard card owns the skill-memory/API boundary even though UMI, VTLA, and Self-Improving Agents each need parts of it.
